title: What is happening with the 231 million euros?
summary: The article analyzes Romania's suspended 231 million euros from the EU Recovery and Resilience Facility due to failure to meet Milestone 215 on special pensions reform. Despite a government delegation to Brussels and the Constitutional Court finally validating the reform law, legal and procedural obstacles remain because the 6-month deadline expired in November 2025 and the law has not yet entered into force.
talking_points: Romania risks losing 231 million euros from PNRR because the special pensions reform milestone was not met within the regulatory 6-month deadline
The European Commission showed leniency by suspending only 231 million instead of the calculated 600 million to 1.4 billion euros, expecting the new government to deliver reforms
Even if the EC accepts a late submission, the European Court of Auditors could later sanction the decision, creating additional legal risk
Milestone 215 requires reform of all special pensions, not just magistrates' pensions, so even if accepted the payment may only be partial
Three scenarios remain: full payment, partial payment for magistrates-only reform, or total loss of the 231 million euros
concepts: legislative entry into force vs adoption
related_concepts: EU conditionality and rule of law
author: Marius Vasiliu
